heading: Limitation on visitorial powers
label: 484 Limitation on visitorial powers
notes: CodificationR.S. § 5240 derived from , , which was part of the National Bank Act. Amendments1982—Subsec. (a). , as amended by , designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), and amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment subsec. (a) read as follows: “No bank shall be subject to any visitorial powers other than such as are authorized by law, or vested in the courts of justice or such as shall be or shall have been exercised or directed by Congress, or by either House thereof or by any committee of Congress or of either House duly authorized”.Pub. L. 97–320Pub. L. 97–457Subsec. (b). , as amended by , added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 97–320Pub. L. 97–457

children.label: a
children.text: No national bank shall be subject to any visitorial powers except as authorized by Federal law, vested in the courts of justice or such as shall be, or have been exercised or directed by Congress or by either House thereof or by any committee of Congress or of either House duly authorized.

children.label: b
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (a), lawfully authorized State auditors and examiners may, at reasonable times and upon reasonable notice to a bank, review its records solely to ensure compliance with applicable State unclaimed property or escheat laws upon reasonable cause to believe that the bank has failed to comply with such laws.
